timeline.notes: Pre-roll. Walking onto mat at Tempo Jiu Jitsu.
Greig (black) and Anthony (red) facing off. About to engage.
Anthony sits to guard. Greig standing, engaging from top.
timeline.coaching_tip: Against seated guard: control feet/ankles before committing weight.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony's feet hooking Greig's leg from below. Not standard guard — legs entangling at lower body. Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? Not yet — Anthony initiating one-sided hook.
timeline.notes: Anthony entering leg entanglement from open guard. Hooking Greig's lead leg.
timeline.coaching_tip: Backstep immediately when you feel leg hooks — don't let the entanglement lock.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? YES — both have one leg wrapped around the other's in a mirror. Q2 both facing same direction? YES. Q3 outside leg over hip? NO — symmetrical. → fifty_fifty.
timeline.notes: 50/50 established. Legs symmetrically entangled. Greig working to get on top.
timeline.coaching_tip: In 50/50: immediately fight for top position. Control the far knee.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? Still mutual wrapping. Q2 same direction? YES. Q3 outside leg over hip? Greig appears to be getting his leg higher/over — transitioning to dominant. → eighty_twenty for Greig.
timeline.notes: Greig gaining dominant position within the leg entanglement. Getting outside leg control. Transitioning from 50/50 to 80/20.
timeline.coaching_tip: Good — use the 80/20 to backstep out into a passing position.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Legs re-tangled after a brief extraction attempt. Q1 mirrored? YES. Q2 same direction? YES. Q3 outside leg? NO → fifty_fifty again.
timeline.notes: Second 50/50 entry. Anthony re-engaging the leg entanglement.
timeline.coaching_tip: Anthony repeatedly pulls you into 50/50. Need to control his feet at the entry point.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Still 50/50. Both legs mirrored. Greig working on top.
timeline.notes: 50/50 maintained. Greig fighting for top.
timeline.coaching_tip: Don't stay here — work the backstep escape.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig getting hips above Anthony. Starting to extract. Outside leg gaining dominance.
timeline.notes: Greig transitioning from 50/50 to 80/20. Gaining dominant control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Now backstep to clear and pass immediately.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig extracting from entanglement. Legs clearing. Mid-pass.
timeline.notes: Greig escaping the leg entanglement. Transitioning to pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Speed is key — pass before Anthony re-establishes guard.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top but one leg still trapped. Half guard.
timeline.notes: Greig in top half guard. One leg still trapped.
timeline.coaching_tip: Crossface and underhook to flatten. Then knee slide.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig perpendicular across Anthony's chest. Legs cleared. Side control.
timeline.notes: Pass complete! Greig in side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: NOW advance to mount within 5 seconds. Don't let the reguard cycle start.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top, heavy. Anthony flat. Side control maintained.
timeline.notes: Side control maintained. Good pressure.
timeline.coaching_tip: Look for americana or advance to mount.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony getting a leg back inside. Half guard recovery.
timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to half guard. First reguard of the cycle.
timeline.coaching_tip: This is the pattern — need faster advancement from side control.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Both separated slightly. Anthony on back with legs between them.
timeline.notes: Open guard. Anthony has recovered. Greig working to re-pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Try a different pass approach — toreando or leg drag.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony more upright with hooks. Butterfly guard position.
timeline.notes: Anthony playing butterfly guard. Greig engaging.
timeline.coaching_tip: Against butterfly: stay low, control knees, deny the underhook.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig one knee splitting. Headquarters passing position.
timeline.notes: Greig in headquarters. Setting up pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Commit to the knee slide with crossface.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top in half guard. Working to re-pass.
timeline.notes: Half guard battle. Greig applying crossface.
timeline.coaching_tip: Win the underhook battle.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig has completed the pass again. On top controlling.
timeline.notes: Second guard pass. Greig in side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: This time go directly to mount — do NOT stay in side control.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top maintaining. Anthony underneath.
timeline.notes: Side control maintained.
timeline.coaching_tip: Advance NOW. Mount or knee on belly.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony getting leg back in again.
timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to half guard. Second reguard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Same pattern repeating. Must advance faster.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Decision tree: Q1 mirrored? Legs entangling again. Anthony pulling Greig into legs. Symmetrical wrapping appearing. → fifty_fifty.
timeline.notes: Third 50/50 entry! Anthony pulling Greig back into leg entanglement.
timeline.coaching_tip: This is a pattern — Anthony defaults to 50/50 when you're in his guard. Address at the entry.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig on top, driving heavy. Anthony flat. Pass complete.
timeline.notes: Greig passes again from the leg entanglement. Side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: ADVANCE immediately. Do not stay.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig knee splitting Anthony's guard. Headquarters.
timeline.notes: Greig in headquarters. Anthony playing open guard.
timeline.coaching_tip: Commit to the pass.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig kneeling over Anthony. Hands on Anthony's legs.
timeline.notes: Open guard exchange. Greig working to pass.
timeline.coaching_tip: Mix up passing — pressure and speed.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Greig driving heavy, close to camera. Perpendicular. Pass complete.
timeline.notes: Another guard pass. Greig in side control.
timeline.coaching_tip: GO TO MOUNT NOW.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony has legs wrapped around Greig again. Closed guard recovered.
timeline.notes: Anthony recovers to closed guard. Greig trapped inside.
timeline.coaching_tip: STAND UP to break closed guard. This is where the sweep came from last time.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony's legs HIGH. Greig being elevated and off-balanced. Clear sweep in execution. Anthony's hips are under Greig, lifting.
timeline.notes: SWEEP! Anthony sweeps Greig from closed guard. Turning point of the roll.
timeline.coaching_tip: The sweep came from broken posture in closed guard — AGAIN. Must stand to break guard.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony on top. Greig underneath. Roles reversed. Anthony consolidating.
timeline.notes: Sweep complete. Anthony on top in side control. Greig on bottom.
timeline.coaching_tip: Immediately frame and hip escape. Don't accept bottom.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony driving heavy on top. Greig flat on back. Side control maintained.
timeline.notes: Anthony maintaining side control with good pressure.
timeline.coaching_tip: Frame on neck and hip. Shrimp away. Get knees inside.

timeline.visual_reasoning: View partially blocked by people walking past. Anthony still on top. Greig still underneath.
timeline.notes: Anthony still controlling from top. View obstructed.
timeline.coaching_tip: Keep fighting for space.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony has shifted. Very tight on top of Greig. Heads appear to be pointing different directions — north-south transition.
timeline.notes: Anthony transitioning to north-south. Heavy control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Frame and spin when they transition to north-south.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony still on top in a tight control position.
timeline.notes: Anthony maintaining dominant top control.
timeline.coaching_tip: Keep creating frames and working for space.

timeline.visual_reasoning: Anthony's weight is forward, sitting on Greig. Greig's legs visible underneath. Anthony has advanced to mount.
timeline.notes: Anthony advances to mount. Greig in worst position of the roll.
timeline.coaching_tip: Trap and roll (upa) or elbow-knee escape immediately.

timeline.visual_reasoning: End of roll. Both on mat. Anthony close to camera.
timeline.notes: End of roll. Greig recovers to open guard.

summary.session_overview: 3:44 open mat roll at Tempo Jiu Jitsu. Greig (black) vs Anthony (red). Three distinct 50/50 leg entanglement exchanges in the first 1:55 (using decision tree: Q1 mirrored legs? YES → Q3 outside leg? NO → fifty_fifty). Greig escaped all three and passed to side control multiple times but couldn't consolidate — Anthony reguarded repeatedly. At 2:40, Anthony swept Greig from closed guard (same vulnerability as identified in previous analysis). Anthony spent the final minute on top advancing through side control → north-south → mount.

key_moments.description: Anthony hooks Greig's leg from seated guard, entering single leg X → 50/50. Decision tree classified correctly: mirrored legs, same direction, no outside leg dominance = fifty_fifty.
key_moments.suggestion: Control Anthony's feet BEFORE engaging. When you feel the hook, backstep immediately.

key_moments.description: Greig transitions from 50/50 to 80/20 by getting outside leg control. Decision tree: mirrored + outside leg over hip = eighty_twenty.
key_moments.suggestion: This is the right escape path. Use the 80/20 advantage to backstep out faster.

key_moments.description: Third 50/50 entry — Anthony keeps pulling Greig back into leg entanglement.
key_moments.suggestion: Anthony's go-to move is 50/50 from guard. You KNOW it's coming — address it preemptively.

key_moments.description: Anthony sweeps from closed guard — same vulnerability as every previous analysis.
key_moments.suggestion: STAND UP in closed guard. This sweep keeps happening because of broken posture on the knees.

key_moments.description: Anthony advances to mount after the sweep.
key_moments.suggestion: After being swept, recovery was too slow. Frame and shrimp immediately.

top_3_improvements: 50/50 defence (decision tree insight): Anthony entered 50/50 THREE times. Using the decision tree: the entry happens when legs become mirrored/symmetrical. Prevent this by controlling feet before engaging seated guard. When you feel BOTH legs wrapping (Q1: mirrored? → becoming YES), backstep BEFORE it locks.

Passing consolidation: Passed to side control 4+ times but never advanced to mount. Side control is a 3-second waypoint. After EVERY pass → mount or knee on belly immediately. Drill: pass → mount within 3 seconds.
Closed guard posture: Got swept from closed guard at 2:40 (same as every previous analysis). STAND UP to break closed guard. Do not stay kneeling with broken posture.
strengths_observed: 50/50 escape using 80/20: Correctly used outside leg positioning to transition from 50/50 to a dominant 80/20 before extracting — shows good leg entanglement awareness
Persistent top pressure: Despite reguards and 50/50 re-entries, kept re-passing. Strong work ethic.
Passing technique: Clean headquarters, knee slide, and pressure passes. The technique is solid.
summary.guard_retention_reason: After sweep at 2:40, gave up positions quickly (side control → north-south → mount). Limited bottom guard work.
summary.positional_awareness_reason: Good awareness in leg entanglements (correctly used 80/20 to escape). Lost points for repeatedly getting caught in 50/50 and closed guard sweep.
summary.transition_quality_reason: Passes were clean but never consolidated to mount. 50/50 escapes were smooth. Post-sweep transitions on bottom were poor.
summary.overall_notes: This v3 analysis with the decision tree correctly identifies the 50/50 entanglements that were previously miscategorised as closed guard. Three key patterns: (1) Anthony's 50/50 game — he pulls you in repeatedly from seated guard. The decision tree shows this is always symmetrical/mirrored wrapping, never sankaku. Prevent by controlling feet. (2) Pass → reguard cycle — 4+ times. Advance to mount immediately. (3) Closed guard sweep — same as every analysis. Stand up. The 80/20 escape at 0:26 was the technical highlight — using the decision tree's Q3 (outside leg over hip) to transition from neutral 50/50 to dominant position before extracting.
